lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Greetings Bwana! I am Trufitus Shakaya of the Tai Bwo Wannai village.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Welcome to our humble village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does Bwana mean?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It means friend, and friends come in peace. I assume that you come in peace?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course I do.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well, that is good news, as I may have a proposition for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A proposition eh? Sounds interesting!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I hoped you would think so. My people are afraid to stay in the village.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: They have returned to the jungle and I need to commune with the gods

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: to see what fate befalls us. You can help me by collecting some herbs that I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me? How can I help?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I need to make a special brew! A potion that helps me to commune with the gods. For this potion, I need very special herbs, that are only found in the deep jungle.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I can only guide you so far as the herbs are not easy to find. With some luck, you will find each herb in turn and bring to me. I will give you details of where to find the next herb.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: In return for this great favour I will give you training in Herblore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds like just the challenge for me. And it would make a nice break from killing things!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: That is excellent Bwana! The first herb that you need to gather is called

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Snake Weed.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It grows near the vines in an area to the south west where

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: the ground turns soft and the water kisses your feet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, sounds difficult, I don't know if I am ready for the challenge.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Very well then Bwana, maybe you will return to me invigorated and ready to take up the challenge one day?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am very sorry, but I don't have any time for that.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Very well then, may your journeys bring you much joy.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Maybe you will pass this way again and you then take up my proposal?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: But for now, fare thee well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am sorry, but I am very busy.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Very well then, may your journeys bring you much joy.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Maybe you will pass this way again and you then take up my proposal?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: But for now, fare thee well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does a warrior like me know about peace?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: When you grow weary of violence and seek a more enlightened path, please pay me a visit

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: as I may have a proposition for you. Now I need to attend to the plight of my people. Please excuse me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tai Bwo Wannai? What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It means 'small clearing in the jungle' but it is now the name of our village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a nice village, where is everyone?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: My people are afraid to stay in the village. They have returned to the jungle. I need to commune with the gods to see what fate befalls us.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You may be able to help with this.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: My greatest respects Bwana, I have communed with my gods and the future

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: looks good for my people. We are happy now that the gods are not angry with us.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: With some blessings we will be safe here.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You should deliver the good news to Bwana Timfraku, Chief of Tai Bwo Wannai. He lives in a raised hut not far from here.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Sorry, Bwana, that herb is so dirty that I can't even tell whether it is fresh. Please clean it first.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Many thanks for the herb Bwana. I'll add it to my collection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hello Bwana. I conclude that you have been successful. Mosol sent word that the village is clearing of zombies. You have done us all a great deed!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Why not go and visit him and have a look around Shilo village. You may find some interesting things there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hello again Bwana! Well Done again for helping to defeat Rashiliyia. Hopefully things will return to normal around here now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Bwana!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Greetings!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I hope things are going well for you now. I have no new information since last we spoke. Needless to say, that if something does come up I will certainly get in touch directly.
